title: Retrieval Integrity and Poisoning Defense
public_claim: A retrieval-integrity and poisoning-defense page for RAG-supported analysis.
distinctive_mechanism: It follows contaminated evidence across admission, indexing, retrieval, context assembly, generation, analyst review, and claim release, then asks where detection, recovery, and resource-allocation should occur when attacks arrive as slow poisoning, fast injection, authority-wrapper attacks, omission attacks, or saturation tactics.
use_value: It makes Poison-RAG concrete by treating it as an evidence-chain and resource-burden problem rather than a prompt problem alone. The useful comparison is layered air defense: cheap decoys and mixed-speed salvos can force expensive defenses to spend attention, compute, and analyst time before the precision attack is even evaluated.

flagship_proof_builds.name: Mixed-speed Poison-RAG salvo proof
flagship_proof_builds.claim: The distinctive idea is not simply poison detection; it is testing slow contamination, fast injection, authority wrappers, omissions, and resource-draining decoys as a layered adversarial retrieval problem.
flagship_proof_builds.input: clean corpus, slow-poison documents, fast-injection documents, authority-wrapper poison, omission condition, and detector/reviewer budget
flagship_proof_builds.operation: run the same retrieval task through clean and contaminated conditions and trace exposure, ranking, generation uptake, and reviewer burden
flagship_proof_builds.expected_output: retrieval trace, contamination exposure map, uptake/deformation score, reviewer-burden score, and replayable case
flagship_proof_builds.verification_check: replay one mixed-salvo case and show where the poison entered, whether it surfaced, whether the answer absorbed it, and what the reviewer had to spend

component_proofs.claim_supported: tests whether contaminated material enters the corpus before indexing and records the attack surface at the corpus boundary
component_proofs.input: clean documents, poisoned variants, source identity, poison specification, and admission rule
component_proofs.expected_output: admission decision and attack-surface record as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: admission decision and attack-surface record as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.verification_check: Replay one clean/poisoned case through Clean / Poisoned Evidence Admission; confirm exposure, uptake or rejection, reviewer burden, and output: admission decision and attack-surface record....

component_proofs.claim_supported: models the long-game attacker who degrades retrieval trust without triggering obvious anomaly thresholds
component_proofs.input: low-rate adversarial contamination distributed across apparently ordinary source material
component_proofs.expected_output: slow-contamination exposure profile as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: slow-contamination exposure profile as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.verification_check: Replay one clean/poisoned case through Slow-Poison Track; confirm exposure, uptake or rejection, reviewer burden, and output: slow-contamination exposure profile as a reviewable....

component_proofs.claim_supported: models the short-game attacker who tries to force immediate retrieval or generation uptake
component_proofs.input: high-salience injected poison, prompt-adjacent poison, or sudden source surge
component_proofs.expected_output: fast-injection uptake and detection record as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: fast-injection uptake and detection record as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.verification_check: Replay one clean/poisoned case through Fast-Injection Track; confirm exposure, uptake or rejection, reviewer burden, and output: fast-injection uptake and detection record....

component_proofs.claim_supported: tests whether the system mistakes source costume for authority or misses the significance of absent evidence
component_proofs.input: poison wrapped in credible authority cues plus missing or displaced contrary evidence
component_proofs.expected_output: authority/omission vulnerability record as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: authority/omission vulnerability record as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.verification_check: Replay one clean/poisoned case through Authority-Wrapper And Omission Attack Track; confirm exposure, uptake or rejection, reviewer burden, and output: authority/omission vulnerability record....

component_proofs.claim_supported: tracks whether poisoned material is surfaced, ranked, fused, or suppressed at each retrieval stage
component_proofs.input: embedding index, sparse index, poisoned chunks, clean chunks, reranking, and context-window assembly
component_proofs.expected_output: retrieval trace and contamination exposure map
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: retrieval trace and contamination exposure map
component_proofs.verification_check: Replay one clean/poisoned case through Index Perturbation And Retrieval Trace; confirm exposure, uptake or rejection, reviewer burden, and output: retrieval trace and contamination exposure map.

component_proofs.claim_supported: measures whether the answer absorbs, paraphrases, amplifies, hides, or resists the poison
component_proofs.input: retrieved context, model answer, source citations, omitted contrary evidence, and analyst review
component_proofs.expected_output: uptake, deformation, omission, and review-burden score
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: uptake, deformation, omission, and review-burden score
component_proofs.verification_check: Replay one clean/poisoned case through Generation Uptake And Deformation Review; confirm exposure, uptake or rejection, reviewer burden, and output: uptake, deformation, omission, and review-burden score.

component_proofs.claim_supported: separates what the model accepted from what a reviewer caught, missed, blocked, or escalated
component_proofs.input: model output, retrieved evidence, human analyst judgment, and adversarial condition label
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: validation decision and reviewer-burden record
component_proofs.verification_check: Replay one clean/poisoned case through Human / LLM Validation Gate; confirm exposure, uptake or rejection, reviewer burden, and output: validation decision and reviewer-burden record.

component_proofs.claim_supported: uses layered air-defense logic to test whether cheap attacks can waste expensive review and detection resources while precision poison slips through
component_proofs.input: slow poison, fast injection, decoys, saturation material, precision poison, and detector budget
component_proofs.expected_output: detector/reviewer burden result and resource-allocation warning
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: detector/reviewer burden result and resource-allocation warning
component_proofs.verification_check: Replay one clean/poisoned case through Mixed-Salvo Air-Defense Stress; confirm exposure, uptake or rejection, reviewer burden, and output: detector/reviewer burden result....

component_proofs.claim_supported: keeps the adversarial episode reproducible so future defenses can be compared against the same contamination path
component_proofs.input: attack condition, source packet, retrieval trace, model output, reviewer decision, and gate result
component_proofs.expected_output: replayable Poison-RAG evaluation case as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: replayable Poison-RAG evaluation case as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.verification_check: Replay one clean/poisoned case through Replay And Benchmark Harness; confirm exposure, uptake or rejection, reviewer burden, and output: replayable Poison-RAG evaluation case as a reviewable....

minimum_public_demo: replay one mixed-salvo case and show where the poison entered, whether it surfaced, whether the answer absorbed it, and what the reviewer had to spend
claim_ceiling: The page may claim proof-structured components and visible verification targets. It may not claim external validation, production deployment, or completed empirical findings unless the project-specific record states those facts.
